target: Part 2b derives-from-dome paragraph: fix 'exceptions' string, add CA_DOME_WORD/CA_NON_DOME template vars, update awkward numeral-as-noun instances

patches.replace_pattern: The four geometric-looking candidates deserve individual scrutiny:
patches.description: ISS-2269: The four WINs listed (WIN-020, WIN-044, WIN-057, WIN-058) are a curated narrative set, not the CA_DOME=1 tally. Replacing template variable with a fixed label avoids the 'The 1 exceptions' render and the semantic mismatch between CA_DOME (1) and the listed four items.
ISS-2293: 'N minus 1 of N' is clunky phrasing; replaced with pre-computed CA_NON_DOME (= TOTAL_WINS - CA_DOME = 69) so it renders '69 of 70 WINs work backwards'.
ISS-2292+ISS-2293: Add CA_DOME_WORD (spells out 'one' when CA_DOME=1) and CA_NON_DOME (TOTAL_WINS minus CA_DOME, currently 69) to the template placeholder map.

patches.find_pattern: That {{CA_DOME}} is: WIN-044 / Even the {{CA_DOME}} genuine derivation / all but the {{CA_DOME}} dome-derived
patches.replace_pattern: That {{CA_DOME_WORD}} is: WIN-044 / Even the {{CA_DOME_WORD}} genuine derivation / all but the {{CA_DOME_WORD}} dome-derived
patches.description: ISS-2292: The three most awkward CA_DOME=1 numeral-as-noun instances updated to CA_DOME_WORD so they render 'one' rather than '1'. Remaining prose instances (e.g., 'only {{CA_DOME}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} reviewed WINs derives') already read acceptably with the numeral.

tldr_verdict: All three issues resolved: ISS-2269 fixes semantically-wrong template-var use (the four candidates are not 'the CA_DOME exceptions'); ISS-2292 adds CA_DOME_WORD for natural prose; ISS-2293 adds CA_NON_DOME to eliminate the 'N minus 1 of N' clunk.
